ddt:Target.Name: EMS3250_C011_0210_V1.0
ddt:Target.Description:
Applications: XHN Engines: HR12VDV ECU: EMS3250 Soft: C011 2XX, _______________________________________________
Reference Documents: DID (RENAULT): b_DIAG_MWXX_PFX_didlistxxx_A DID (SUPPLIER): MD07 DTC: b_DIAG_MWXX_PFX_eventlistx_A_C0110210
Items_Bases_DDT
Starting Point: EMS3250_C011_210_V1.0 _______________________________________________ EMS3250_C011_0210_V1.0(MBL):

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Item.Text: FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
The base DTC provides the complete description of the failure
No Bus Signal / Message Specific SubType
Circuit Short to Ground or Open
Circuit Short to Battery or Open
Current Flow Below a Specified Range
Current Flow Above a Specified Range
Circuit Resistance Below a Specified Range
Circuit Resistance Above a Specified Range
Electrical Conditions detected during a few moment
Signal Amplitude Below a Specified Range
Signal Amplitude Above a Specified Range
Signal remains Low when Transitions are Expected
Signal remains High when Transitions are Expected
Improper Signal Shape (plot of Amplitude with Respect to Time)
Circuit which is Superimposed a Signal Voltage
Low Pulse is too Narrow with Respect to Time
Low Pulse is too Wide with Respect to Time
High Pulse is too Narrow with Respect to Time
High Pulse is too Wide with Respect to Time
Incorrect Number of Cycles in a Given Time Period
Mismatch between Hardware Connected and Hardware Expected
Internal Temperature Above a Specified Range
Some Part of the Program has not been Enabled
Some Part of the Program has been Disabled
Need to Program Sub System (or Vehicle) Option Content
Two (or More) Input Parameters compared for Plausibility
Function actived for Greater than a Specified Time Period
Fewer Transitions than a Specified Number
More Transitions than a Specified Number
Correct Change of parameter(s) in Response to a Particular Event has not been detected
Detection of a System Event that was not caused by System itself
No Motion Detected in response to Energizing the Component
No Motion Detected in response to Closing the Component
No Motion Detected in response to Opening the Component
Excessive Duration in response to Energizing the Component
Unable to Move the Component to the Emergency Position
Unable to Move the Component to the Commanded Position
Incorrectly Adjusted or Aligned Component Detected
Actuator Operational but Driven Device not Operating
Component has an Unexpected Gas (or Liquid) Flow
Fluid Level too Low for Proper Operation
Validity Bit equal to 'invalid' or Post Processing Invalid
Corresponding Rolling Count Value being unproperly Updated
Message with an Incorrect Protection Calculation
Circuit Quantity Below a Specified Range
Circuit Quantity Above a Specified Range
Circuit Quantity Not Plausible given to Operating Conditions
Component Parameter Outside its Expected Range
Component Performance Outside its Expected Range
Component Operating in an Incorrect Way
Component Operation Prevented by an Obstruction
COOLANT TEMPERATURE SENSOR FOR INTERCOOLER OUTLET
MASS AIR FLOW/TEMPERATURE/HUMIDITY ALL-IN-ONE SENSOR (SINGLE INTAKE) air flow
Mass or Volume Air Flow Sensor "B"
COOLANT TEMPERATURE SENSOR FOR ENGINE OUTLET
2 CELLS TYPE UNIVERSAL EXHAUST GAS OXYGEN SENSOR HEATER
DOWNSTREAM EXHAUST GAS OXYGEN SENSOR HEATER
Rich to lean transition for upstream oxygen sensor
Lean to rich transition for upstream oxygen sensor
Low Pressure Fuel Pump Control Relay
downstream high pressure exhaust gas recyclation temperature sensor
Upstream lean Nox trap temperature sensor
Downstream Lean Nox Trap Temperature Sensor
TURBINE PRESSURE SENSOR FOR INLET OF TURBINE
Engine system lubrication of Remote Engine Start
LOW PRESSURE FUEL PUMP CONTROL RELAY
1st stage of ECM driver of the injector
2nd stage of ECM driver of the injector
Upstream SCRF NOx Sensor - Nox measurement stage
Downstream SCRF Nox sensor - NOx measurement stage
NORMALLY HIGH NEUTRAL SWITCH BY WIRE
14V DC-to-DC electrical converter (direct current) to (direct current)
High-Voltage system Insulation resistance failure level 2
Direct Current of Charge High Voltage connector Interlock
upstream selective catalytic reduction temperature sensor
Upstream SELECTIVE CATALYST REDUCTION NOx sensor
2 CELLS TYPE UNIVERSAL EXHAUST GAS OXYGEN SENSOR
Downstream SCRF Nox sensor - O2 measurement stage
GPF removal diagnosis based on pressure difference
GPF removal diagnosis based on temperature difference
Upstream SCRF Nox sensor - 02 measurement stage
Downstream SCRUF Nox sensor - Nox measurement stage
Downstream selective catalyst reduction Nox sensor 2
Downstream SCRUF Nox sensor - O2 measurement stage
Upstream selective catalytic reduction under floor temperature sensor
By Pass Exhaust Gas Recirculation Valve Position
Downstream Selective Catalytic Reduction under floor temperature Sensor
Particulate Matter sensor - regeneration phase of the electrode
Mastervac vacuum pump, braking assistance system vacuump pump
Pumping current of upstream 2 cells oxygen sensor
Belt-driven Starter Generator (BSG 12V) from PT CAN
Electrical Power Steering from PT CAN
Adas information system from PT CAN
Advanced Emergency Braking System from PT CAN
Electrical Torque Splitter Wheel Torque Request
Body Control Module (BCM) Air conditioning (AC) Min Engine Idle Speed Request
Air conditioning (AC) Min Engine Idle Speed Request
Vehicle motion control (VMC) Wheel Torque Max Limit Request
ADAPTATIVE CRUISE CONTROL (ACC) engine stop auto order
Automatic gearbox torque loss-Invalid Serial Data Received

Data.Name: Oxygen sensor monitoring (YES : complete , NO: not complete)
Catalyst monitoring (YES: complete , NO: not complete)
DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter_2
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCLastDiseappearMileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCTravelledDistanceFailurePresent
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCFirstAppearMileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.IUPRnumerator
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.IUPRdenominator
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData(use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCRecord.IUPR
Data.Comment: Status for the IUPR using service $19

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Item.Text: rscr - Particulates filter regeneration for SCR needs
Test routine for UDS implementation validation
Command for SED activation, thermic engine stopped

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Item.Text: Position setpoint of the inlet throttle sent by the monitoring system
Final position setpoint for multiways valve
PWM control applied to the driver of the exhaust VVTC solenoid valve
Activation request solenoid valve of additional fuel tank
Activation request pump of additional fuel tank
Flow water valve command of water system
Catalyst exhaust gas upstream oxygen resistance heater PWM command
Catalyst exhaust gas downstream oxygen resistance heater PWM command
PWM value for the sensor heater
Exhaust Air Flap - ET throttle postion setpoint used by the monitoring system
Low Pressure EMTV - EGR BP valve control setpoint used by the monitoring system
Swirl Flap - Swirl throttle postion setpoint used by the monitoring system
PWM command asked to the Hbridge
Position setpoint used by the monitoring system
State of the fuel pump relay control
Command of the drain cut valve for the EVAP diagnosis
Command relay state of the fuel heater
Water pump water charge command state
State of rear motor-fan thermic activation request
EGR Water Cooling Pump PWM Command
Permanent inhibition of Stop and Start function with After sales tool
Final pwm send to the scr pump
Very low speed engine fan delayed command
Final pwm value send to automatic gearbox waterpump
Low temperature cooling loop water pump PWM command
Pwm command of the second EGR cooler bypass
Heater driver PWM duty cycle, acquired also by BSW upper
Heater driver PWM duty cycle, acquired also by BSW lower

Data.Name: IOCTL.DataIdentifier
Data.Comment: Data Identifier received in reply to InputOutputControl request

Oil pressure pump proportional final command

egr water cooling pump 2 pwm command

Last mileage activation request of DFC protection

HP TCR bypass valve position setpoint after software limit stops
HP TCR position setpoint after software limit stops

Stop and Start Gen2 system inhibition causes merged into an array of 4 bytes.
Number of Auto Start applicable for BSG conf
Stop and Start External Failure 1 (with auto start) causes merged into an array of 6 bytes - first 4 bytes.
Stop and Start External Failure 2 (with manual start) causes merged into an array of 4 bytes.
Absolute time (used for PID $4D and $4E calculation)
Absolute time in second (Used for Timer ExtendedData Management calculation)
Total number of first engine starts
Total number of first engine starts. Non-resettable
Total number of engine starts. Non-resettable
Stop and Start AutoStop request inhibition causes merged into an array of 4 bytes.
Counter of HV Relays connection cycles
Odometer distance memorized value when going in power cut while in power save.
Consolidated state indicating activation of bench mode
The snapshot DID Auto Start Impossible
Stop and Start Internal Failure 2 (without auto start) causes merged into an array of 2 bytes.
Regulation loop activation request for EEM needs
Alternator slope number sent to alternator
Voltage rate limitation requested by Powertrain

BSG internal failures flag for recranking mode diagnosis.

Activation of light commercial vehicles idle speed setpoint.

Inhibition of StopAuto by a diagnosis tool request

Flag for torque assist ready request

Air conditioning compressor clutching request sent from AC ECU
After sale tool request to inhibit power save downgraded mode
This configuration is to define the presence of the turbo charger electrical water pump
Final result for the Oil Pressure Routine
Boolean Vector containing conditions to trigger the double flywheel protection secure strategy

NORMALLY HIGH NEUTRAL SWITCH BY WIRE

upstream selective catalytic reduction temperature sensor

Vehicle motion control (VMC) Wheel Torque Max Limit Request

Label.Text: Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
Danc ce cas le calculateur est déprotégé et n'interdit pas le démarrage moteur
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est protégé car il ne reçoit pas les trames de la BCM: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est protégé car la BCM ne répond pas correctement: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> La BCM n'a pas authentifié la clé ou le badge
-> L'ECM n'est pas monté sur le véhicule sur lequel il a appris le code
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est vierge et protégé car la BCM n'envoie pas la clé secrète: l'ECM n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> La BCM n'est pas autorisée à envoyer la clé secrète
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM
Immobilizer - engine not running due to BCM in secure mode
Immobilizer - engine not running due to no BCM authorisation
Immobilizer - engine not running due to a CAN network problem with the BCM
L'ECM est "autoprotégé" et n'autorise pas le démarrage moteur.
-> L'ECM a subit une tentative "d'intrusion" ou a détécté une erreur interne (EEPROM, FLASH...)
Readiness code completion PID 1 octet A + B
Readiness code completion PID 1 octet C + D
DATA BYTE B: On-board diagnostic evaluation and Continuous monitoring tests
DATA BYTE D: Status of tests run at least once per trip
Readiness code completion PID 1 octet B BIT B5/B6 - octet D BIT B7
DATABYTE B, BIT 6: CC monitoring readiness
DATABYTE D, BIT 7: EGR system monitoring
BIT 7: Reserved (bit shall be reported as '0')
Test complete or not applicable = 0 soit diag joué
Test not complete = 1 soit diag non joué
DATA BYTE A: Number of DTC and MIL lamp status
Number of memorized emission-related powertrain failures
DATABYTE B, BIT 5: Fuel system monitoring readiness
DATA BYTE D: Status of test run at least once per trip
DATA BYTE C: Supported tests run at least once per trip
Learning state of the TDC sensor

Button.Text: Read O2 Sensor diag status - EU5

Display.DataName: Catalyst monitoring (YES: complete , NO: not complete)
Oxygen sensor monitoring (YES : complete , NO: not complete)
Oxygen sensor monitoring (YES : complete , NO: not complete)
Oxygen sensor monitoring (YES : complete , NO: not complete)

Label.Text: States description for softwares in LM >=40
Sonde à 2 cellules de Nernst, e.g.BOSCH LSU ADV
States description for softwares in LM<40
sonde mono-cellule ex DENSO ou BOSCH LSU 5.1
Bench mode to apply default on
Numerator and denominator for IUPR private classes
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.01
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.01
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.02
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.02
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.03
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.03
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.04
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.04
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.05
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.05
Numerateur des IUPR pour les classes private.06
Denominateur des IUPR pour les classes private.06

Take care that the correspondance Index - Component described in this screen is not always respected. Please check the TdH and calibration in order to know exactly the correspondance between Index and Component!
Parameters linked to the switching in Stop Auto
Functionnal State of the vehicle with Stop and Start
Clutch pedal : End of stroke - wire
Clutch pedal : Beginning of stroke - wire
Cumulative number of engine start for the starter
Minimum travel condition to go in Automatic Stop
Vehicle will not moving (P or N +EPKB)
Clutch pedal : Very beginning of stroke - wire
Clutch pedal : Consolidated status for end of stroke
Brake pedal states received on the CAN
Cumulative number of engine starts for the HP pump
Maximum number of activation of starter to inhibite Stop and Start
Driver conditions to go in Automatic Stop
Vehicle speed conditions to go in Automatic Stop
Clutch pedal : Beginning of stroke - CAN
Clutch pedal : End of stroke - CAN
Clutch pedal : Beginning of stroke - wire
Very begin of stroke sensor activation state
Gear Lever position received on the CAN
Displaying of Stop and Start request
Stop and Start system failure cases
Manual range M+ and M- in AT
Bit 5: Stop and Start inhibited by ECM
Bit 12: Automatic or manual parking brake detected
Bit 15: Engine stop origine: emergency request
Bit 1: System conditions to go in automatic stop
Bit 2: Auto stop forbidden by BCM
Bit 8: Auto stop forbidden by powersteering system
Bit 13: Stop auto request aborted because of brake pressure conditions not reached
Bit 15: Stop auto forbidden due to energy management
Maximum duration the engine can stay automatically stopped
Ambiant pressure high threshold below which auto stop is forbidden
Ambiant pressure low threshold below which auto stop is forbidden
Minimum speed threshold to go to StopAuto after a deactivation of 4WD function
Maximum delay to confirm a StopAuto request by the driver (by braking pressure)
Delay before prompting auto stop MMI
Maximum slope value to authorize StopAuto (negative value)
Maximum slope value to authorize StopAuto (positive value)
Delay to detect rear gear engaged
Maximum vehicle speed to keep the engine automatically stopped
Delay to confirm that the vehicle is stoped (consolidation of slope value)
Vehicle speed threshold to validate the minimum travel conditions
Vehicle speed threshold to validate the minimum travel conditions in rear detected
Vehicle speed threshold to authorize automatic stop
Gear Lever position received on the CAN
Bit 14: Autostart sequence failed by system
"1" value - not OK (interdiction or failure detected)
Bit 0: Vehicle speed information validity status
Bit 2: BeginStroke clutch information validity status
Bit 3: EndStroke clutch information validity status
Bit 4: Neutral contact information validity status
Bit 5: Reverse contact information validity status
Bit 6: HV battery (RSA) or battery sensor (Nissan) info validity status
Bit 7: SAS disabled because of higher starter activation counter
Bit 8: Consolidated braking pressure information validity status
Bit 9: Brake pedal information validity status
Bit 10: Lever position information validity status
Bit 11: Road slope information validity status
Bit 13: Nissan ICR relay information validity status
Bit 14: Nissan IS relay information validity status
Bit 3: State of ECUs for S&S inhibition
Bit 8: Driver seat state information validity status
Inhibition of Stop Start by a diag tool request
Vehicle with key or keyless vehicle
Configuration - Clutch pedal - Minimum travel switch
Configuration - Brake pedal - open active switch
Activation or Desactivation of ABSMalfunction for StopAuto inhibition
Idle Stop System failure judgment conditions
AUTO Start/Stop I/O Control By DID ($2F)
I/O Control $24E5- Boost pressure PWM command (gasoline)
I/O Control $2414 - Throttle valve position setpoint
I/O Control $281C - Gas pump command state
I/O Control $2033- Intake camshaft phaser PWM command
I/O Control $2188 - State of the CTP unicing
I/O Control $2262 - Activation request solenoid valve of additional fuel tank
I/O Control $2263 - Activation request pump of additional fuel tank
I/O Control $2822 - CONTI - Canister drain valve command
I/O Control $224D - PWM control applied to the driver of the exhaust VVTC solenoid valve
I/O Control $2822 - VALEO/BOSCH - Canister drain valve command
AUTO Start/Stop I/O Control By DID ($2F)
I/O Control $2927 - Motor driven fan setpoint
I/O Control $24BF - Turbo water cooling pump command state
I/O Control $2871 - Piloted thermostat PWM command
I/O Control $2264 - Flow water valve command of water system
I/O Control $2167- Oil pump monitored command
I/O Control $244B - Catalyst exhaust gas upstream oxygen resistance heater PWM command
I/O Control $244C - Catalyst exhaust gas downstream oxygen resistance heater PWM command
I/O Control $28C7 - Air flaps PWM command
I/O Control $2887 - PWM of fuel pump actuator
I/O Control $2408 - EGR valve position setpoint
I/O Control $246E - EGR cooling bypass PWM command
I/O Control $2882 - State of the fuel pump relay control
I/O Control $292B - Activation voltage stabilizer request
I/O Control $2512 - PWM value for the sensor heater
I/O Control $25BF - Request for starter relay 1
I/O Control $20F8 - Final position setpoint for multiways valve
I/O Control $2A37 - PWM command of the second EGR cooler bypass
I/O Control $221C - Cooling pump set point
I/O Control $2A19 - Final PWM value sent to automatic gearbox waterpump
I/O Control $20FB - Demand of heater valve flow
I/O Control $2B69 - Monitool idle speed setpoint.value

Screen.Name: Adaptatifs de richesse - Sonde proportionnelle - Lecture / Ecriture

Label.Text: Indicator of UEGO sensor signal first gain correction achieved when sensor is changed
Apprentissages lents (s'apprend au fur et à mesure )
Filtered adaptive value for the first value of the TDC counter in the second speed range (ns.rpm)
Parameters linked to the switching in stop auto
Minimum travel condition to go in automatic stop
Driver conditions to go in Automatic Stop
Functional I State of S & S supervisor
Functional I State of S & S Coordinator
Max number of activation of HP Pump to inhibit Stop&Start
Cumulative number of engine starts for the HP pump
Max number of activation of starter to inhibite Stop&Start
Cumulative number of engine start for the starter
IF1: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 1 (with auto start)
EF1: EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type1 (with auto start)
EF2: EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type2 (without auto start)
EF3: EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type3 (wear of starter)
IF2: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 2 (without auto start)
IF3: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 3 (wear of starter)
bit1 = Brake Vacuum failure (validity of signal)
bit16 = Driver Seat Belt Buckle state failure
bit17 = Driver Seat Belt failure state failure
bit21 = Under Starter Relay condition failure
bit18 = STCUT_STRLY Condition failure (validity of signal)
IF2: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 2 (without auto start)
IF1: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 1 (with auto start)
IF3: INTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 3 (wear of starter)
EF1 : EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 1 (with auto start)
EF2 : EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 2 (without auto start)
EF3 : EXTERNAL FAILURE DETECTION Type 3 (wear of starter)
StopStart LEVEL1 Override (DevMode must be activated)

Cumulative number of engine start for the starter

bit8 = Braking pressure (DMLN) or P or N + PKB
S&S LEVEL 1 Override (DevMode must be activated)
Cumulative number of engine start for the starter
Inhibition of Stop Start by a diag tool request
Cumulative number of engine starts for the HP pump
